127217.explanation: We stand for quality instead of selection based on gender. Our two current aldermen are also happy to continue working in the coming years for an even more beautiful Wierden. We choose continuity with them.

127218.explanation: NEW is open to an advisory (non-binding) referendum. Whether it is used depends on the topic. In Wierden, residents are already widely involved in new plans and there is plenty of room for input. Think of our future vision, neighborhood and road design, and playgrounds.

127221.explanation: Together with our farmers, we are working on the future. Not every location is suitable for a campsite. Together with them and other residents, we ensure that no uncontrolled growth occurs and that farmers can maintain or obtain an income model.

127222.explanation: NEW gladly supports initiatives from residents through advice and assistance.

127223.explanation: We develop business parks for our own entrepreneurs. We must guard against urban sprawl in our landscape.

127226.explanation: Given the limited scale, we see no reason to ban the use of fireplaces and wood stoves.

127228.explanation: NEW regrets that the tradition of fireworks will disappear after 2026. Together with residents, we will think about how we can shape our New Year's Eve traditions. As far as we are concerned, there should be more room for shooting carbide.

127230.explanation: Events are closely monitored to ensure compliance with noise standards. We see no reason to tighten these standards.

127231.explanation: The municipality is not financially responsible for the interior design of entrepreneurs' buildings. However, the municipality provides support and advice.

127232.explanation: NEW supports developing cluster farms when a farmer stops. Preferably, we build homes on the existing farmyard and not on fertile agricultural land.

127235.explanation: The municipality already subsidizes music education in Wierden. All young people can take music lessons at a reduced rate.

127237.explanation: Wierden recently appointed an extra enforcement officer (BOA). Given the growth of our municipality in recent years, this was also necessary. At present, further expansion is not needed.

127239.explanation: NEW wants all women to have a safe home. If there is demonstrable need, we are not opposed. Cooperation with other Twente municipalities should be sought in this.

127242.explanation: Given the housing crisis, NEW has no objection to vacation homes being used as permanent residences. National policy will have to be followed.

127243.explanation: Building outside the current village boundaries must always be carefully considered; NEW does see opportunities for this. Farmyards are also suitable provided the agricultural sector does not experience inconvenience.

127244.explanation: NEW believes that participating in society makes residents happier and more vital. Once people are able to perform work, it can be beneficial for them to follow a daily routine and start working again.

127246.explanation: This is already 120% of the social assistance level in Wierden. That is sufficient for now.

127248.explanation: Many charging points have been realized in recent years. The municipality encourages charging on private property as much as possible. When expansion is needed, a careful consideration must be made, as enough regular parking spaces must remain available.

127249.explanation: In Wierden, the decision has already been made to move towards 30 km/h within built-up areas. Roads will be adjusted accordingly in the coming years.

127250.explanation: The quality of care and the ability to provide care to everyone who needs it is our top priority. Care providers can consider clients' needs when this is possible with staffing.

127251.explanation: With the property tax (OZB), the municipality funds facilities not reimbursed by the national government. Think of sports, meeting places, swimming pools, and the development of roads and playgrounds in neighborhoods. NEW takes the position that the OZB is used transparently and will not rise unnecessarily.

127254.explanation: Development based on need and in consultation with business associations. Currently, plots have been designated for business park expansion in both Wierden and Enter. We primarily provide space for local entrepreneurs.

127256.explanation: NEW believes that status holders should not get priority for social rental housing. Our own residents who have been on the waiting list for years should have priority.

127258.explanation: NEW supports school swimming. Unfortunately, there is no political majority for this. Financing does not have to lie with the municipality. Parents pay for swimming lessons that take place during school time (PE classes) together with classmates—win-win.

127259.explanation: Education itself determines what is paid attention to besides the normal curriculum. There are various initiatives that support the Twente dialect at schools. We value that the Twente language and customs do not disappear.

127262.explanation: NEW wants to preserve the village character, which does not include tall residential buildings. However, we recognize that space is scarce, and therefore building up to 4 or 5 stories is no problem for us, provided it fits in the environment.

127263.explanation: We are in favor of renting according to demand. Renting out single rooms and splitting homes are also options.

127264.explanation: For homes without a private driveway, it is important that there are enough parking spaces in the neighborhood. We will keep an eye on this.

127266.explanation: NEW supports the retention of a swimming pool in the municipality of Wierden. A study is currently being conducted on the future of De Kolk swimming pool. Based on need, financial resources, and developments around us, we will make an appropriate choice in the interest of residents.

127267.explanation: NEW does not want large wind turbines in the municipality of Wierden. The effects of large wind turbines on the health of our residents are unclear. In addition, large wind turbines could slow down housing development, and we also find these turbines do not fit within the landscape.

127217.explanation: We primarily value quality in forming the executive board. Gender is not decisive. A balanced reflection is desirable, but the suitability of candidates comes first.

127218.explanation: Participation is valuable to us, but the municipal council has received a democratic mandate through elections to make decisions.

127221.explanation: We want to facilitate such developments where possible, but not without conditions. Initiatives must fit within existing policy frameworks.

127222.explanation: The CDA believes in shared responsibility and local ownership. Energy cooperatives strengthen the community and create support. Therefore, we support initiatives from residents, provided they are safely, feasibly, and carefully integrated into the living environment.

127223.explanation: In our opinion, large distribution centers do not belong in our municipality. We do consider small-scale variants possible.

127226.explanation: Clean air is essential for our health, especially for vulnerable groups. The CDA does not opt for bans but for awareness and personal responsibility. Through good information and restraint during unfavorable weather conditions, we work together towards a healthier living environment.

127228.explanation: Safety and health come first. The CDA observes increasing damage, injuries, and disturbances. By choosing central or alternative celebrations, we preserve the tradition of celebrating together while limiting risks for residents, emergency services, and animals.

127230.explanation: Events are important for vibrancy but must not come at the expense of residential and living quality. The CDA opts for clear rules and customized solutions per situation, with attention to local residents and organizers.

127231.explanation: Accessibility is a joint responsibility. Everyone, including people with disabilities, should be able to participate. Entrepreneurs are crucial, but adjustments cost money. The CDA therefore wants to support with subsidies and advice, without extra regulatory burdens.

127232.explanation: The CDA recognizes the need but wants to prevent cluttering of rural areas. Tailored solutions are necessary within clear frameworks. Initiatives that contribute to the vitality of the countryside deserve space, provided they are landscape- and legally responsible.

127235.explanation: The CDA values equal opportunities but chooses targeted support. Help must be available for families who need it.

127237.explanation: Safety requires more than just extra enforcement. The CDA chooses an integrated approach with prevention, cooperation, and visibility in the neighborhood. Additional enforcers are only needed if they demonstrably contribute.

127239.explanation: An appropriate facility must be available, preferably in cooperation with surrounding municipalities.

127242.explanation: Permanent residence undermines recreation and spatial planning. The CDA chooses clarity: holiday homes are intended for recreation. Housing shortages are solved with targeted housing construction, not with shortcuts.

127243.explanation: The CDA wants to use the rural area sparingly. New construction should primarily take place in or adjacent to existing centers. Only in exceptional cases and with clear added value is tailored consideration possible.

127244.explanation: As far as personal circumstances permit, we find it desirable that social assistance recipients perform socially meaningful work.

127246.explanation: We are strong proponents of supporting residents with financial worries. Recently, progress has been made towards the legal maximum. Further increases are currently not possible because this maximum has been reached.

127248.explanation: At present, the number of charging stations is sufficient in relation to the number of electric cars. Therefore, no additional designations are necessary.

127249.explanation: Traffic safety and livability demonstrably improve at lower speeds. The CDA supports 30 km/h zones where it fits the street layout and the safety of vulnerable road users.

127250.explanation: Care close to home is often better and more efficient. The CDA wants to strengthen good local facilities. Freedom of choice is important but must not come at the expense of quality and affordability of care in the municipality.

127251.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the property tax (OZB) as low as possible, but reduction is only responsible if facilities are maintained. Financial stability and investments in the community come first.

127254.explanation: Space is needed for a healthy economy. The CDA wants to timely develop new business parks, focused on local companies and sustainable design, so that employment is preserved.

127256.explanation: The CDA stands for mercy and justice. Priority causes tensions in a tight housing market. We choose small-scale shelter and fair distribution.

127258.explanation: The CDA emphasizes the importance of school swimming and water safety. Primary responsibility lies with parents and education. The municipality can support and facilitate, for example through good swimming facilities and cooperation with schools, but is not primarily responsible.

127259.explanation: Culture and tradition are meaningful to us. Where possible, we see a stimulating role for the municipality to promote attention to these at primary schools.

127262.explanation: We are open to more multi-story buildings but only at a limited height of about three to five floors, fitting the character of our municipality.

127263.explanation: Uncontrolled room rentals can lead to nuisance and displacement. The CDA chooses regulation and protection of livability in neighborhoods.

127264.explanation: Current parking standards align with the required parking capacity (CROW standard) and therefore provide sufficient space. Further expansion is not necessary.

127266.explanation: Research is currently underway into the future of the swimming pool. We do express a clear preference for retaining a swimming facility in our municipality.

127267.explanation: Sustainability is important, but health and safety come first. The CDA supports wind turbines only at demonstrably safe distances from buildings and with local support.

127217.explanation: Equal representation of men and women ensures diverse perspectives and fairer decision-making. A balanced composition of the executive board contributes to inclusivity, recognizability, and trust in local government.

127218.explanation: Referendums give residents direct influence on important decisions and increase involvement, support, and trust in local democracy. They complement the municipal council, provided everyone can participate, is well informed, and they concern major, guiding issues.

127221.explanation: Farmers must have space to make their businesses future-proof. Side activities such as shops or campgrounds strengthen the local economy and short supply chains and can contribute to a more sustainable countryside, provided basic rules for livability and environment apply.

127222.explanation: Citizen initiatives for sustainable energy accelerate the energy transition and increase local involvement. Municipal support makes participation possible for everyone and ensures that benefits are fairly distributed and remain local.

127223.explanation: New distribution centers put pressure on landscape, space, and livability and cause extra traffic. By limiting their establishment, the municipality can protect nature and choose sustainable, locally rooted employment.

127226.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves cause air pollution and health complaints for neighbors. The municipality must prioritize clean air and health and limit their use, with attention to feasible alternatives for residents.

127228.explanation: A central fireworks show offers a safe and shared alternative to private fireworks. This reduces nuisance, injuries, and damage and ensures a festive celebration everyone can participate in.

127230.explanation: Stricter noise standards protect the health and living comfort of residents while allowing events to continue. This way, a good balance is found between vibrancy, culture, and livability.

127231.explanation: Accessible buildings ensure everyone can participate fully in society. By financially supporting entrepreneurs with adjustments, the municipality promotes inclusion and prevents accessibility from depending on financial means.

127232.explanation: There is understanding for farmers' desire to build homes, for example for succession or care, but this must be done carefully. The municipality must be able to steer on landscape, nature, affordability, and livability. Tailored solutions and clear rules are needed to prevent cluttering.

127235.explanation: Music education contributes to the development and creativity of children. By subsidizing this, the municipality ensures that all primary school children, regardless of income, have equal opportunities to take music lessons.

127237.explanation: More municipal enforcers can contribute to safety and livability by being visibly present, identifying issues, and acting preventively. Combined with good support and social prevention, they help prevent problems early and avoid escalation.

127239.explanation: Domestic violence occurs everywhere. In smaller municipalities, seeking help is often harder due to social control. A shelter, small-scale or regional, provides quick, safe refuge and guidance. Safety must not depend on municipal size.

127242.explanation: Permanently living in holiday homes can be a solution to housing shortages for some, but must not lead to problems with livability, facilities, or nature. The municipality must provide tailored solutions and set clear conditions, with attention to housing quality and fair housing distribution.

127243.explanation: Building outside village boundaries puts pressure on the landscape, nature, and space. By mainly building within existing villages, the open countryside is preserved and the focus is on compact, livable villages with good facilities and less fragmentation.

127244.explanation: People on social assistance are entitled to support and trust, not coercion. Mandatory social work often does not help in finding paid employment and can lead to extra pressure and stress. Voluntary participation with good guidance works better and is more respectful.

127246.explanation: By raising the income threshold, people with low to middle incomes who just fall outside existing schemes can also receive support. This increases subsistence security, prevents debt, and ensures that help better matches reality.

127248.explanation: More charging points make electric driving accessible for more residents and support the transition to cleaner transportation. By expanding public charging facilities, the municipality ensures equal access, including for people without a private driveway.

127249.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h increases traffic safety, reduces noise nuisance, and makes streets more pleasant for pedestrians and cyclists. It contributes to livable neighborhoods and a safe environment, especially for children and the elderly.

127250.explanation: Good care is tailored to the individual. Freedom of choice for a care provider that fits someone's faith, culture, or beliefs increases trust, accessibility, and quality of care. Everyone should feel safe and respected when receiving care.

127251.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is an important source of income to keep public facilities affordable. Reducing it may seem attractive but must not come at the expense of care, education, and livability. The rate should be fair and balanced, with attention to ability to pay.

127254.explanation: Employment is important, but more business parks also put pressure on space, landscape, and livability. The municipality should first better utilize and make existing sites more sustainable, and weigh on a case-by-case basis whether expansion is truly necessary and appropriate.

127256.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit have the right to a good start, but priority must not be at the expense of other urgent housing seekers. Housing should be allocated based on urgency and need, with attention to fair distribution and social support.

127258.explanation: School swimming increases children's safety and ensures equal opportunities, since not all parents can afford swimming lessons. By financing this, the municipality invests in health, prevention, and safe development of children.

127259.explanation: Attention to the Twents dialect contributes to culture, identity, and language diversity. By enabling this in primary schools, the local language remains alive and children learn respect for their environment and heritage.

127262.explanation: Higher residential buildings can help absorb housing shortages and save space, but must fit the scale and character of Wierden. The municipality must evaluate each location for spatial and social desirability, with attention to livability, views, and greenery.

127263.explanation: Individual room rentals can lead to over-proliferation and pressure on neighborhood livability. The municipality should focus on good, independent, and affordable homes and protect residential quality and cohesion in neighborhoods.

127264.explanation: Additional parking spaces can reduce pressure in neighborhoods but often come at the expense of green and play areas. The municipality should focus on tailored solutions: parking where needed, combined with better alternatives such as shared mobility, cycling facilities, and good public transport.

127266.explanation: Closing Zwembad De Kolk and completely rebuilding is expensive and can lead to a long-term loss of swimming facilities. The municipality should rather invest in preservation, renovation, and sustainability so swimming remains accessible and affordable for residents.

127267.explanation: Wierden should contribute to sustainable energy generation. At the same time, Progressive Wierden wants to discuss with the province to regain control, so the municipality itself can steer location choices, participation, and protection of local residents. This keeps local interests leading.

127217.explanation: Diverse teams generally perform better. This applies to gender, but also to age, for example. An equal number of men and women is not a goal in itself.

127218.explanation: There are many opportunities to participate in the municipality. Council members are approachable and communication about (planned) proposals is clear. A referendum is not an effective instrument.

127221.explanation: The municipality has an important role in supporting farmers. The municipality's mindset should be positive.

127222.explanation: The municipality can facilitate and possibly help with a small subsidy. The municipality itself does not play an active role.

127223.explanation: There is no place for this in our municipality.

127226.explanation: Wood burning can cause health damage, so we should be cautious with it. However, much can be prevented by burning properly.

127228.explanation: The municipality knows the tradition of carbide shooting. A fireworks show (for example, in collaboration with entrepreneurs) could be a nice addition.

127230.explanation: There should be room for parties in the municipality. This must be in good consultation and according to the applicable rules which should be well enforced.

127231.explanation: This is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs. The municipality can support with advice.

127232.explanation: This is possible via the red-for-red scheme. The current scheme works well.

127235.explanation: There is financial support for low-income households so that music lessons are possible for every child.

127237.explanation: Safety is an important aspect to maintain our society. Recently, more community enforcement officers (boa's) have been appointed.

127239.explanation: There must always be room for women who need a safe place. If necessary, that could be in Wierden.

127242.explanation: Permanent residency comes at the expense of holiday parks. However, the homes can be used for temporary bridging.

127243.explanation: There are good rules and regulations for this (Red-for-red). With these rules, this is occasionally possible. This is balanced by the demolition of old barns to prevent clutter.

127244.explanation: If possible, a counter-performance may be requested.

127246.explanation: The current limits (usually 120%) of the social assistance standard are fine.

127248.explanation: The number of electric cars is growing, so the number of charging points must grow accordingly. This should happen simultaneously.

127250.explanation: There must be sufficient room to make choices in this.

127251.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is an important source of income for the municipality. Important facilities are paid from it. The Christian Union opposes unnecessary increases, but a reduction is also not an option. Wierden already has a favorable rate compared to other municipalities.

127254.explanation: New business parks have been designated. There is a need for this development, but afterwards it is not logical to create more business parks.

127256.explanation: The municipality has a target to house status holders. If they do not have priority, another way must be sought. In practice, this concerns a relatively small group of tenants.

127258.explanation: In many cases, schools do not have time to reintroduce school swimming. The municipality can support children in low-income families to pay for swimming lessons. The Christian Union is very pleased with this scheme. It is very important that all children learn to swim safely.

127259.explanation: If schools want to, there are lesson packages available for this. The municipality does not have an active role in this.

127262.explanation: High-rise flats do not fit the character of the municipality. However, it is possible to examine whether there are locations where rules for high-rise buildings can be somewhat relaxed to address the housing shortage.

127263.explanation: Subletting is already possible under certain conditions. This can be a (temporary) solution if someone needs housing.

127264.explanation: The neighborhoods in the municipality generally meet the parking standards. If shortages do occur, a customized solution can be considered that preferably also allows for more green space.

127266.explanation: There is a need in the municipality for a good and sustainable swimming pool. It is not yet clear whether it is cheaper and better to build a new pool or to renovate De Kolk.

127267.explanation: Wierden has so far prevented any cooperation on wind turbines. As a result, the province has taken over all control. The Christian Union wants to take back this control and be able to choose where wind turbines are placed themselves. The location along the A35 near Zuidbroek is very unwise.

127217.explanation: It is about quality; the most capable people.

127218.explanation: We are a relatively small municipality that is very accessible. Residents can think along and participate through participation processes and various channels.

127221.explanation: "Always" is a big word, because there will be locations where this does not fit in the zoning plan.

127222.explanation: There are national "funds" that provide for this. The municipality should not override these.

127223.explanation: Of course it depends on who, what, where, and how. So it depends on the circumstances.

127226.explanation: Not enforceable. There are no real complaints here.

127230.explanation: The question does not start from a baseline measurement. You are simply allowed to produce certain decibels.

127231.explanation: Not financially, but certainly facilitating. Helping with a permit, for example.

127232.explanation: With the rules that exist, this is possible. There are game rules and everyone must adhere to them.

127235.explanation: This should be stimulated by, among others, Kaliber, which is supported by the municipality. In fact, this is already subsidized. Naturally, children of parents with low incomes can make use of the available facilities.

127237.explanation: Safety is paramount, but we are a relatively safe municipality, and with three enforcement officers (BOAs), enforcement is well organized.

127239.explanation: This is a GGD (Public Health Service) matter. In Wierden this is minimal, so it can be handled regionally.

127242.explanation: The holiday parks in our municipality are established as holiday parks. They are socially and economically anchored in the area.

127251.explanation: This must be kept as low as possible. Purely inflation correction and nothing more.

127254.explanation: Fortunately, two are already being added. Work is underway. There is a need.

127256.explanation: No, we simply follow the rules.

127258.explanation: Practically no longer feasible. Big question marks regarding education.

127259.explanation: The municipality does not interfere with the content of education.

127262.explanation: We will have to. Everything in proportion.

127264.explanation: There is a need for it. There are increasingly more cars.

127266.explanation: In the long term, this is an option for Wierden.

127267.explanation: Our municipality is unsuitable for large wind turbines.

127217.explanation: Not necessarily, it is about the best administrators.

127218.explanation: Not necessarily through a referendum, that is quite heavy. There are sufficient opportunities to express opinions or have input.

127221.explanation: Farmers must have the freedom to safeguard their livelihoods in these complicated times for them. Caveat: small-scale campsites such as 'camping at the farmer's' are allowed. A farm shop is always a welcome addition.

127222.explanation: Such initiatives must be supported. Of course, according to safety standards and rules.

127223.explanation: Large industrial halls do not fit the village character of our municipality in our opinion. We see much more potential in high-tech or specialized companies, both for image quality and certainly for employment.

127226.explanation: Although nuisance can occur, this is something you cannot take away from the enthusiast. Also keep in mind that it can sometimes be financially motivated.

127228.explanation: Fireworks is actually a very strange tradition. It originated 2000 years ago in China to ward off evil spirits. Although fireworks can be beautiful to watch, we believe this is not a task for the municipality.

127230.explanation: Complaints about noise nuisance occur at practically every festival, especially on Sundays. We would greatly appreciate if events would not be held on Sundays, and if they are, that consideration is given to church services on that day.

127231.explanation: That depends on the situation at that company. People with disabilities should fully participate in social life in our view. In principle, a company should arrange this itself, but in exceptional cases the municipality could provide support.

127232.explanation: Provided that an additional company residence is possible. Also, demolishing old structures and building small starter homes instead should be allowed. We do not mean that all farmers' land may be fully built up with houses.

127235.explanation: Fortunately, this already applies to children of parents with lower incomes. For children from other income groups, subsidies are not necessary in our opinion.

127237.explanation: The BOAs (special enforcement officers) obviously already do this, but reinforcement is welcome. As SGP we strive for maximum safety.

127239.explanation: That would be a good option to accommodate people in need. Churches can play a good complementary role here.

127242.explanation: This would be a good measure to relieve housing pressure. However, this must take place based on clear agreements made in advance.

127243.explanation: Only when infill development is no longer possible in the proper manner is expansion outside the existing village boundaries inevitable.

127244.explanation: But only if the person concerned is capable of this and the home situation permits.

127246.explanation: The earlier poverty is addressed, the better.

127248.explanation: This should be further encouraged, although there is the question of whether there is capacity in the power grid.

127249.explanation: A lower speed causes accidents less quickly and, in case of an accident, usually results in less injury than at 50 km/h. The relevant road sections should be appropriately adapted for this.

127251.explanation: Only if it is responsible and possible. We do believe that the internal budget should first be reviewed to identify possible savings. Only then is raising taxes potentially a last resort. We are very critical of this.

127254.explanation: Work is being done on that.

127256.explanation: We believe that our youth and starters have priority over status holders.

127258.explanation: That would be a good option, as in the past. This benefits the swimming safety of our children.

127259.explanation: Be proud of our regional language. It embodies our culture.

127262.explanation: We protect the village character of our municipality but sometimes cannot avoid somewhat higher buildings due to housing shortage. In our opinion, new construction with a maximum of three floors is allowed.

127263.explanation: That entirely depends on the building and the environment. This requires a case-by-case approach.

127264.explanation: In some neighborhoods yes, in others there is no parking problem.

127266.explanation: We are firmly for retaining a swimming pool in the municipality, make that clear! However, we want to make a responsible choice between renovating swimming pool De Kolk or building a new one at a different location in our municipality. In both cases, these are major investments.

127267.explanation: Large wind turbines do not fit in the beautiful and low-wind Wierden. Furthermore, it has been shown that this is not feasible due to distances, restrictions, and nature areas. As SGP, the well-being of our residents takes priority over generating sustainable energy. However, we do encourage farmers' windmills.

statements.title: Wierden should agree to the installation of wind turbines in the municipality.

titleParts.text: Wierden moet akkoord gaan met de plaatsing van

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Wierden should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The residents of Wierden should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Wierden moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: More tall apartment blocks should be allowed in Wierden.
In Wierden the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves must remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Wierden moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Wierden must have a women's shelter (a refuge for victims of domestic violence).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: Wierden must have more business parks.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to Twents.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must close Zwembad De Kolk and build an entirely new swimming pool.
